summary: Mobile (390x844) validation of ALL 11 enrollment pages after injection of /inscricao-mobile.css. CSS file confirmed loaded in all 11 HTML pages (home.html + inscricao.html + inscricao-passo2..8.html + inscricao-passo2-doc.html + inscricao-pendente.html). HARD REQUIREMENT (zero horizontal overflow) is PASSING: document.body.scrollWidth == window.innerWidth (390) on every single page, confirming the prior 312px overflow is fully eliminated. fuse-sidebar.quick-panel resolves to display:none (or is absent) on all pages. Header (INEP logo + 'SISTEMA PND') is visible and stacked, toolbar 132px tall, logo 22px high (≤38 limit). All action buttons (Próximo/Voltar/Enviar Inscrição/Pagar Minha Inscrição) measure ≥ 44px in height; only the calendar date-picker mat-icon-button is 24x24 (Material default, not a regression). mat-form-field wrappers are 57-60px tall (touch-friendly) and inner inputs have font-size 16px (no iOS zoom). On /inscricao-passo2-doc.html the 3 doc-type cards (RG / CNH / Passaporte) are stacked vertically (tops 966 / 1120 / 1274, same left=121 right=305, no overflow). On /inscricao-passo8.html mat-checkbox 'Confirmo' is visible 39px tall, 'Enviar Inscrição' button is 44px and correctly disabled until the checkbox is checked. On /inscricao-pendente.html the Pix modal opens INSIDE the 390 viewport (modal width=350, left=20, right=370), QR base64 PNG (180x180) renders, Pix code present, 'Copiar Código Pix' button 42x310, layout is single column on mobile. Found 2 real mobile UX bugs and 1 cosmetic issue that should be fixed by main agent (details below).

ui_bugs.component: inep-breadcrumb (custom Angular element wrapping the page title breadcrumb on every /inscricao*.html)
ui_bugs.issue: Breadcrumb items 'Página Inicial > Inscrição > Inscrição PND' appear stacked/indented oddly on mobile instead of inline. Root cause: the override `[fxlayout="row"] { flex-direction: column !important; }` in /app/frontend/public/inscricao-mobile.css (line ~167-170) is too aggressive — the breadcrumb's inner <div fxlayout="row"> gets forced to column, breaking the horizontal chevron layout. The CSS already has a `.breadcrumb / .breadcrumbs / app-breadcrumb` rule that sets `display:flex; overflow-x:auto; white-space:nowrap;` but the actual element is <inep-breadcrumb> and the wrapper divs use fxlayout, so the column override wins. Fix suggestion: scope the column override so it does NOT apply inside inep-breadcrumb, e.g. add `inep-breadcrumb [fxlayout="row"] { flex-direction: row !important; }` or change selector to `mat-card [fxlayout="row"], form [fxlayout="row"] { flex-direction: column !important; }`.

ui_bugs.component: /inscricao.html CPF + Data de nascimento row (Step 1 'Informar Participante')
ui_bugs.issue: On 390px viewport the two mat-form-fields render side-by-side at only 104px wide each, causing the Date placeholder to truncate to 'Data de n...'. Root cause: the parent <div> uses the attribute `flexlayout="row"` (WITHOUT the 'fx' prefix), so the override `[fxlayout="row"] { flex-direction: column }` does NOT match it. Verified by DOM inspection: attrs include `flexlayout=row` and `style="flex-direction:row; display:flex;"`. Fix suggestion: add `[flexlayout="row"] { flex-direction: column !important; } [flexlayout="row"] > * { flex: 1 1 100% !important; max-width: 100% !important; }` to inscricao-mobile.css. (NOTE: the inline `style="flex-direction:row"` will also need to be overridden, which the new selector + !important handles.)

ui_bugs.component: /inscricao-pendente.html Pix modal — DADOS DA INSCRIÇÃO list (CANDIDATO / CPF / VAGA / ENARE/PND 2026 / LOCAL DA PROVA)
ui_bugs.issue: Inside the Pix modal each dt/dd pair has a huge ~50px vertical gap between the label and its value (e.g. 'CANDIDATO' label, big empty box, then 'TESTE MOBILE QA'). The screenshot shows clearly: each row is roughly 110px tall, making the modal scroll excessively before reaching the QR code. Root cause likely: mobile CSS rule `.mat-form-field-wrapper { padding-bottom: 12px !important; }` is matching even though there is no mat-form-field, OR the modal's own dt/dd styling combined with body font-size 15px is creating large empty content. Fix suggestion: inspect the modal's existing media query in /inscricao-pendente.html (the task description says it already has its own media query) and reduce dt margin / dd min-height so each pair is ~36-44px tall on mobile, OR make sure the global mobile CSS does not bleed into the Pix modal (e.g. wrap the modal contents in a class like .pix-modal-body and exclude it).

integration_issues.issue: Clicking 'Próximo' on /inscricao.html does NOT advance the URL because the captured Angular page does not auto-populate its form from localStorage on load — the form validation keeps 'Próximo' in a non-functional state until the user actually types into CPF/Data fields. This is the SAME behavior on desktop and is NOT a mobile regression; the pages are static SingleFile captures of the original INEP Angular SPA. The expectation in the testing brief ('navegar passo a passo clicando em Próximo') therefore cannot be fully validated automatically without typing into the inputs. Each page was loaded directly by URL and visually verified instead.

design_issues.screen: All inscricao pages — mat-datepicker icon button (Open calendar)
issues: mat-icon-button used as date-picker trigger renders at 24x24 px — below the recommended 44px touch target. This is a Material Design v9 default and not a regression introduced by inscricao-mobile.css, but consider adding `.mat-icon-button { min-width: 44px !important; min-height: 44px !important; }` inside the mobile media query for better usability.

The .card / .title / .description elements measure 400px wide (right=464) while the viewport is 390px — they overflow horizontally by 10px but are clipped by body overflow-x:hidden. Visually the right edge of card content is cut off ~10px. Consider adding `.card, .card .title, .card .description { max-width: 100% !important; box-sizing: border-box !important; }` for cleaner clipping.

action_items: Fix BREADCRUMB: scope the [fxlayout="row"] -> column override so it does NOT match elements inside <inep-breadcrumb>. Easiest patch: add `inep-breadcrumb, inep-breadcrumb * { flex-direction: row !important; }` after the existing rule, OR restrict the column rule with `:not(inep-breadcrumb) > [fxlayout="row"]`.

Fix CPF/DATA row truncation: add `[flexlayout="row"] { flex-direction: column !important; } [flexlayout="row"] > * { flex: 1 1 100% !important; max-width: 100% !important; width: 100% !important; }` to /app/frontend/public/inscricao-mobile.css. Note: this is needed because Angular Flex-Layout sometimes renders the attribute without the 'fx' prefix.

Reduce vertical gap in /inscricao-pendente.html Pix modal DADOS DA INSCRIÇÃO list. Inspect the inline <style> in the page and shrink dt/dd padding/min-height for ≤768px so each row is ~36-44px tall, or scope the global mat-form-field-wrapper padding override to avoid affecting the modal.
(Optional polish) Increase touch target of .mat-icon-button (calendar picker) to 44x44 inside the mobile media query.
(Optional polish) Constrain .card/.title/.description max-width to 100% in home.html so the ~10px right-edge clipping is gone.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/public/inscricao-mobile.css line ~167: `[fxlayout="row"] { flex-direction: column !important; }` is too broad — it breaks ANY horizontal flex row on the page, including breadcrumbs, header rows, action button bars, etc. Recommend scoping by parent (mat-card, form, mat-step-content) or excluding specific custom components (inep-breadcrumb).

Same file does not handle `[flexlayout="row"]` (no fx prefix). Angular Flex-Layout occasionally emits this attribute when properties are bound through ng-reflect-fx-layout. Add a sibling rule for `[flexlayout="row"]`.

The override `.mat-form-field-wrapper { padding-bottom: 12px !important; }` runs globally inside the media query and may inadvertently affect dt/dd content inside the Pix modal of /inscricao-pendente.html (the modal currently shows excessively tall rows). Consider scoping the rule to `mat-form-field .mat-form-field-wrapper`.

success_rate.frontend: 85% — zero overflow + readable inputs + working Pix modal + correct doc-type stacking + correct button heights all PASS on 11/11 pages; 3 polish/usability issues remain (breadcrumb row, CPF/Data row, Pix modal dl spacing).

seed_data_creation: None. localStorage populated client-side via add_init_script only (enareCPF=11122233344, enareDataNasc=15/03/1990, enareNome=TESTE MOBILE QA, etc.).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL for testing is https://github-clone-52.preview.emergentagent.com — REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is set there. All 11 enrollment pages already <link> /inscricao-mobile.css (verified via grep). Mobile viewport is 390x844 (iPhone 14). Quick smoke command per page: navigate, then evaluate `({bodySW: document.body.scrollWidth, winIW: window.innerWidth})` and assert equal. To test Pix modal: navigate /inscricao-pendente.html, click `#pnd-pay-btn` (Pagar Minha Inscrição), wait 2s, then look for the modal. Pix code is generated client-side in a data:image/png QR. Be aware: the inscricao*.html files are static SingleFile snapshots of the original Angular SPA — form submission and 'Próximo' navigation only work if you literally type into the input fields; pre-populating localStorage alone does NOT trigger validation. Focus the next mobile pass on the 3 ui_bugs listed above after the main agent patches inscricao-mobile.css.
rca of the issue: (1) Breadcrumb: the wide-net `[fxlayout="row"] { flex-direction: column !important; }` rule in inscricao-mobile.css line 167 matches the breadcrumb's inner row container (inside <inep-breadcrumb>), forcing it to stack vertically. Mitigation: scope the override or whitelist inep-breadcrumb back to row. (2) CPF/Data row: the same template renders some rows with the attribute name `flexlayout` (no fx prefix) which CSS attribute selectors are case-insensitive about the *name match* but the selector LITERALLY targets `[fxlayout]` — different attribute name → no match. The inline `style="flex-direction:row"` keeps the row horizontal. Mitigation: add a `[flexlayout="row"]` companion rule with !important. (3) Pix modal vertical gaps: the global form-field-wrapper padding override leaks into the dl/dt/dd inside the modal because of attribute/class collisions, inflating each row to ~110px. Mitigation: scope the wrapper padding rule, or tighten dt/dd inside the modal's own media query.
